chapter_goal: Make Hamiltonian G-spaces, comoment maps, orbit spaces, reduction preview, classical momentum maps, and coadjoint orbits executable.

source_span_read.title: Hamiltonian Actions
source_span_read.source_note: Inspected the local PDF text for moment/comoment maps, orbit spaces, the C^n reduction preview, SO(3) coadjoint orbits, linear and angular momentum, and the coadjoint-orbit homework.

library_routing.representation: definition dependency graph plus exact Poisson bracket checks
library_routing.why: the two moment-map axioms and the comoment bracket axiom are structural dependencies
bad orbit spaces are best seen as collapsed reachable sets with separation failures marked
moment fibers, zero level, and half-space image are radial numerical objects
the maps y -> -y and (x,y) -> x cross y are vector-valued and spatial

library_routing.representation: SO(3) coadjoint spheres plus KKS rank/kernel checks
library_routing.why: the orbit is a sphere and the Poisson/KKS checks are exact algebra

visual_sequence.inspection_target: follow the two moment-map axioms into the comoment bracket condition
visual_sequence.validation: Poisson bracket for coordinate functions on so(3)* has zero Jacobiator

visual_sequence.inspection_target: see which orbits collapse and why the fixed/unstable point is hard to separate
visual_sequence.validation: orbit ledgers distinguish Hausdorff and non-Hausdorff models

visual_sequence.inspection_target: connect radial moment map, zero level sphere, quotient, and half-space image
visual_sequence.validation: unit sphere samples have mu=0 after choosing constant 1/2

visual_sequence.inspection_target: compare translation momentum with rotation angular momentum
visual_sequence.validation: angular Hamiltonian contraction matches d<a, x cross y>

visual_sequence.concept: SO(3) coadjoint orbit and KKS form
visual_sequence.inspection_target: read coadjoint spheres as symplectic leaves of the Lie-Poisson bracket
visual_sequence.validation: KKS kernel is the stabilizer direction and tangent rank is two
